QF.id: VB Fund Distribution and Channel Management_5

question.text: Which organization issues the ARN (AMFI Registration Number) to mutual fund distributors?

answer.text: AMFI (Association of Mutual Funds in India)
SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India)

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** AMFI (Association of Mutual Funds in India) is the industry body that issues the ARN to mutual fund distributors. AMFI is the self-regulatory organization of the mutual fund industry.
**The Trap:** While SEBI regulates the mutual fund industry, it is AMFI that handles distributor registration and issues ARN numbers. RBI and NSE have different roles altogether.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in Series V-A. Remember: NISM gives the certification, AMFI gives the ARN.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** After passing the NISM exam, you need to apply to AMFI for your ARN. Keep your AMFI registration details updated — especially your contact information and KYC documents.
